method.step_1: Represent each truck-day with stop-zone, GPS image, route distance, and fleet metadata.
method.step_2: Build stop-profile vectors and route-image clusters as evidence.
method.step_3: Ask the LLM to reason over statistical evidence and produce final pairwise truck correlations.
method.step_4: Normalize positive LLM-reasoned target-to-other correlations into truck appearance frequencies.
method.step_5: Sample target route clusters by target route frequency and other-truck routes by joint frequency.
method.truck_appearance_frequency: Positive target-to-other LLM-reasoned correlations normalized across other trucks.
method.route_appearance_frequency: Cluster days divided by selected period length.
method.joint_scenario_frequency: LLM truck appearance frequency multiplied by route appearance frequency.

1247.llm_rationale: Zone4/7/2 behavior overlaps, but distance intensity, purpose, and age differ.

1311.llm_rationale: Negative stop relation and very different purpose/intensity.

1312.llm_rationale: Stop profile has some similarity, but daily distance and purpose are very different.

1350.llm_rationale: Same mobile-equipment role and partial zone overlap, but operating intensity and age differ.

1363.llm_rationale: Both are short-distance with low zone4/7/2 activity, despite different purposes and ages.

1421.llm_rationale: Same mobile-equipment role, but 155 is much shorter-distance and older with lower zone volume.

1423.llm_rationale: Negative stop relation and very different short mobile-equipment versus long production use.

1506.llm_rationale: Route-image similarity is not enough to overcome different duty cycle, purpose, and operations.

1575.llm_rationale: Kms similarity and some zone overlap are outweighed by purpose, age, and distance-intensity differences.

1576.llm_rationale: Same mobile-equipment role and shared zone2/4/7 behavior, but 155 is much shorter-distance and older.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Same purpose/duty; age and kms weaker.

1578.llm_rationale: Some stop similarity, but production long-haul and mobile-equipment short-distance work differ strongly.

1667.llm_rationale: No stop-profile alignment and different purpose/intensity; only duty and some kms support.

1687.llm_rationale: Both are low-distance trucks, but purpose, age, and detailed zone behavior differ.

1688.llm_rationale: Same mobile-equipment purpose, but stop profile is negative and operations differ.

1785.llm_rationale: Same mobile-equipment purpose, but negative stop profile and different speed/distance patterns.

1993.llm_rationale: Stop profiles align around low zone4/7/2 activity, but purpose, age, and kms differ.

llm_feature_interpretation.fleet_metadata: Duty matches; purpose and metadata weak.

2191.llm_rationale: Some stop overlap but very different short-distance mobile-equipment versus long-haul production use.

2201.llm_rationale: Zone4/7/2 behavior overlaps and both are lower-intensity than production, but purpose/age differ.

2518.llm_rationale: Stop profile overlaps, but 155 is much shorter-distance and different purpose/age.

final_scenario.sampling_method: For each scenario day, sample the target truck route from its own route_appearance_frequency, then sample one other truck route from joint_scenario_frequency = truck_appearance_frequency * route_appearance_frequency.
